title: pimelea tinctoria
intro: Pimelea tinctoria, also known as yellow die or native yellow-top, is a shrub native to Australia and parts of Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and a history of traditional use. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its ability to soothe and heal wounds. The health benefits of Pimelea tinctoria include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Pimelea tinctoria can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, infusions, and topical ointments, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
